quote_of_day.text:
>>55583
That orwell quote is so true
It's not that women are more competent than men, it's just that they're more servile and see no problem in brown nosing to get the trust of the ones above them, without caring about autonomy and freedom.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of racial paranoia moved into the board this morning, with a thread questioning the role of a perceived entity in filtering the white race.

The discussion drew a flurry of responses, with one anon suggesting it was an effort to purge 'inferior filth.' A scattered high-pressure ridge of historical debate formed over the steppe ancestry and rebellious streak discussion.

Anon referenced various regional groups and their histories, drawing connections between ancestry and rebelliousness. Meanwhile, a brief but intense squall of accusations developed in another thread, with one anon claiming thread deletion and sparking defensive responses.

A cool front of concern swept over the board in the afternoon, with anon expressing worries about internship and job prospects.

A separate drizzle of anecdotes formed around the story of Sonam Wangchud, who reportedly returned to their hometown to work for the local community after studying abroad.

The board's mood remained overcast, with discussions veering between conspiratorial and mundane topics.

articles.title: Anon Questions Blacked Psyop's Role in Filtering White Race
articles.body:
Anon initiated a discussion on whether the Blacked psyop is being pushed to eliminate inferior elements from the white race. The original post suggested that such efforts help filter out 'filth' and promote quality over quantity within the white race.

One respondent agreed, citing Nick Fuentes and arguing that women are objects by nature and should be used and discarded if they do not serve a purpose.

This anon suggested that women who are 'dysgenic, dark-skinned, or have a body count over 0' can be discarded.

Another anon proposed using collective intellect and numbers to create a world where women are rewarded for serving men, suggesting they will adapt to such a society.

This poster advocated for a strict social hierarchy where women are excluded from politics and the internet. A different anon expressed agreement with the idea that women are not intelligent and should not participate in politics or medicine.

This poster also suggested that women are hedonistic and should be used accordingly. One poster also brought up the influence of Jews in modern feminism, citing Gloria Steinem as an example of a CIA agent who spied in India.

This anon concluded that women are stupid and referenced 'Jeetland' as evidence of female stupidity. The discussion also touched on the idea of consent and marital rape, with one anon arguing that consent is a fictional term and women are property.

This poster claimed that other faiths also view women as property to be used.

Anon also argued against the concept of equality and women's rights, suggesting that an ideal society would exclude women from politics, the internet, and require strict virginity checks for unmarried women.

This poster cited data to support the idea that all-male job forces perform better than mixed or all-female ones.

articles.title: Anon Debates Steppe Ancestry and Rebellious Streak
articles.body:
Anon opened a discussion on the intertwined nature of steppe ancestry and a rebellious streak, citing historical examples of Bhumihar Ranveer Sena and Khalistani Bhinderwala.

Another anon responded with a seemingly unrelated and obscene remark about a past relationship, which did not contribute to the initial topic.

A respondent questioned the hype around steppe ancestry, stating that genes originated from a single point and new ones will emerge in the future. This anon expressed disinterest in validating their existence based on steppe ancestry.

In a different direction, an anon expressed a desire to have children who carry their name, faith, and principles. This respondent saw no deeper meaning beyond being a mammal.

One participant differentiated between Jaats from Rajasthan and Jatts from Punjab, with the former described as more patriotic, intelligent, and soft-spoken. Anon also brought up the Tamil Tigers as a modern and effective fighting force in the subcontinent.

A critic of steppe obsession argued that it likely did not come from male ancestors but could have been introduced through female lines.

The topic of regional identity and dehatism was raised, with one anon stating they wanted North India to not be associated with certain states.

The discussion also turned to the point of living, reproducing, and loving one's family, history, and heritage, with one respondent suggesting these values are significant. Some exchanges became personal, with insults and derogatory terms used between respondents.

articles.title: Anon Claims Thread Deletion, Sparking Accusations
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread saying, "He deleted the thread yaaro". Other Anon responded with what appeared to be a reference to a different conversation: "Because the CP poster posts when I am asleep.

There's no CP posting when I am online. I'm not neeting here 24/7. I come here when I am bored and keep checking catalog for any illegal stuff.

You can carry on the mental masturbation though." Another Anon chimed in, writing: "If you are dyaush, I'd like to apologise, you knew your shit, bhach was 100 times better than whatever the fuck this is.

Pajeets are not ready for free speech." A respondent shot back: "We know it's you, dyaush.

Your shitass larp and trying to larp with new typing style isn't very effective." An accusation was made that a user, referred to as FP, was of Pakistani origin and attempting to "infiltrate indian internet".

One Anon wrote: "Wouldn’t be surprised tbh. FP definetly was Paki." This led to a request to "denounce Allah", to which an Anon replied: "Allah is a randi ka pilla".

The thread took a religious turn, with one participant writing: "Yaar i am not even muslim, why do you both haggus all shit on every thread".

Tensions escalated, with an Anon stating: "i hate you now" and accusing another of being "islamophobic like haldilal". A retort came: "Everyone is Islamophobic here, its just common sense".

articles.title: Anon Worries About Internship and Job Prospects
articles.body:
Anon expressed concern about their lack of internship or job prospects as their seventh semester is about to start.

Another anon suggested that companies often prefer candidates with stronger academic backgrounds, such as those who cracked JEE Advanced at a young age, when hiring for roles with a CGPA requirement.

One respondent advised Anon to consider a master's program, mentioning that some T5 schools offer fully funded programs with opportunities to teach or assist in research.

Anon also received advice to explore job opportunities in PSUs, which have GPA cutoffs, and to research M.Tech programs, which may provide better prospects.

articles.title: Anon Recounts Sonam Wangchud's Story
articles.body:
Anon shared a story about Sonam Wangchud, an alumnus of Nit Srinagar, who went to Europe for higher studies, returned to his hometown, worked for the people there, and asked for government accountability.

He was labelled an ISI agent, released a year later, and eventually died on hunger strike.

Anon criticised Education Minister Chodi for not taking accountability, citing several educational fiascos including JEE and NEET paper leaks. Others defended Chodi, saying he was immune to abuse and had support.

Some users exchanged insults, with one calling another a 'low IQ gutter ki paidaish'.
